subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following is the best evidence that a potted plant is a living thing?

options.B: It can manufacture food and respond to light
options.C: It is fixed to one position
options.D: It contains water in its tissues

answer_text: It can manufacture food and respond to light

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Living things carry out life processes such as nutrition, respiration, growth, excretion, movement or response, and reproduction.
steps: Being green alone is not enough to prove life because some non-living objects can also be green.
A living plant manufactures food by photosynthesis and also bends or grows toward light, showing response to stimuli.
Being fixed in one place does not mean it is non-living; many plants are stationary but alive.
The option that combines real life processes is therefore the correct one.
explanation.final_answer: B. It can manufacture food and respond to light
explanation.simple_explanation: A plant is recognized as living not merely by appearance but by the life activities it performs, especially nutrition and response. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: A student touched the leaf of Mimosa pudica and the leaflets folded up immediately. This shows

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Irritability or sensitivity is the ability of a living thing to detect and respond to changes in its environment.
steps: The folding of Mimosa leaflets happens after touch, which is a stimulus.
The plant detects that stimulus and responds almost immediately.
This is not nutrition, excretion, or reproduction.
It is a clear example of irritability, also called sensitivity or response to stimuli.
explanation.simple_explanation: When an organism notices a change and reacts to it, that life process is irritability. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of living things?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Living things show common life processes, whereas crystallization is a property of some non-living substances.
steps: Respiration, growth, and excretion are standard characteristics of life.
Crystals can increase in size, but that increase is not biological growth.
Crystallization is a physical process in non-living matter.
Therefore it is not a characteristic of living things.
explanation.simple_explanation: JAMB often tests the difference between true biological growth and non-living increase such as crystal formation. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following processes occurs in both plants and animals as a sign of life?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Respiration is a universal process in living cells used to release energy from food.
steps: Plants and animals both need energy for life activities.
That energy is released through respiration in their cells.
Photosynthesis and manufacture of chlorophyll are restricted to green plants and some other organisms, not animals.
Locomotion from place to place is not shown by all plants.
explanation.simple_explanation: Both plants and animals respire, even though they do not obtain food in the same way. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: A boy says that a car is living because it moves. The best reply is that movement alone does not prove life because a car

options.C: does not carry out metabolism or reproduction

answer_text: does not carry out metabolism or reproduction

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Movement by itself is not enough to define life; living things must perform coordinated metabolic processes and other life functions.
steps: Cars can move, but that movement is produced externally by mechanical systems and fuel combustion.
A car does not respire biologically, grow by cell division, reproduce its own kind, or excrete metabolic wastes.
Living things show internal organization and metabolism.
Therefore movement alone is not sufficient evidence of life.
explanation.final_answer: C. does not carry out metabolism or reproduction
explanation.simple_explanation: Many non-living things move, so JAMB likes asking for the deeper features that truly define life. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following is the best example of excretion in a mammal?

options.B: Sweating out urea, water and salts

answer_text: Sweating out urea, water and salts

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Excretion is the removal of metabolic waste products produced by cells.
steps: Sweat contains water, salts, and a little urea, all of which are removed from the body.
Faeces mainly represents undigested food and is removed by egestion, not excretion.
Chewing is digestion, while taking in oxygen is part of breathing.
Therefore sweating out metabolic wastes is the correct example.
explanation.final_answer: B. Sweating out urea, water and salts
explanation.simple_explanation: Excretion and egestion are different. JAMB often tests that distinction directly. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: The increase in dry mass of a seedling over several days is the best indication of

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: True biological growth is a permanent increase in size and dry mass due to synthesis of new protoplasm and cells.
steps: Dry mass increase means new living material has been formed.
This is different from merely taking in water, which may enlarge an object temporarily.
Crystallization is a non-living process.
Hence the change described is true growth.
explanation.simple_explanation: Growth is not just getting bigger; it is a lasting increase in living material. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following statements best distinguishes growth in a living organism from increase in size of a crystal?

options.A: Both involve increase in surface area only
options.B: Growth in organisms is internal and irreversible, while crystal increase is external accretion
options.C: Crystals increase only in mass, not in size
options.D: Organisms grow only when placed in water

answer_text: Growth in organisms is internal and irreversible, while crystal increase is external accretion

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Biological growth occurs from within through cell division and assimilation, while crystals enlarge by deposition of particles on their outside.
steps: In organisms, new cells and protoplasm are formed from inside the body.
In crystals, particles simply attach to the outer surface from the surrounding medium.
That is why crystal enlargement is not regarded as true biological growth.
The best distinction is internal irreversible growth versus external accretion.
explanation.final_answer: B. Growth in organisms is internal and irreversible, while crystal increase is external accretion
explanation.simple_explanation: This is a classic JAMB comparison question: living growth happens from inside; crystal increase happens by deposition on the outside. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: A housefly lays eggs which hatch into maggots. The process illustrated is

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Reproduction is the production of new individuals of the same kind.
steps: The eggs develop into young stages of the same organism.
That means the organism has produced offspring.
The question is not about feeding, breathing, or response.
Therefore the life process shown is reproduction.
explanation.simple_explanation: Whenever new individuals are formed, the process is reproduction. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following is the best example of nutrition in a green plant?

options.B: Manufacture of glucose from CO₂ and H₂O in the presence of light
options.C: Loss of water vapour through leaves
options.D: Bending of the shoot toward light

answer_text: Manufacture of glucose from CO₂ and H₂O in the presence of light

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Nutrition in green plants mainly refers to autotrophic feeding through photosynthesis.
steps: Green plants make organic food from simple inorganic raw materials.
Carbon dioxide and water are converted into glucose in the presence of light and chlorophyll.
The other options describe respiration, transpiration, and response respectively.
So the correct example of plant nutrition is photosynthesis.
explanation.final_answer: B. Manufacture of glucose from CO₂ and H₂O in the presence of light
explanation.simple_explanation: For plants, nutrition usually points to photosynthesis in UTME Biology. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following pairs consists only of life processes?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Life processes are activities carried out by living things to maintain life.
steps: Growth and excretion are both standard life processes.
Rusting and combustion are chemical changes in non-living matter.
Evaporation is a physical process and diffusion can occur in living and non-living systems.
Therefore only option B contains two definite life processes.
explanation.simple_explanation: A good exam strategy is to ask whether the process is specifically biological or just general physical/chemical change. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: A yam tuber kept in a store room later produces shoots. This is evidence mainly of

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: The formation of new shoots from a stored tuber shows renewed development and vegetative propagation.
steps: The bud begins to divide and enlarge, which is growth.
The shoot can give rise to a new plant of the same kind, showing vegetative reproduction.
Although respiration is also occurring, it is not the main evidence highlighted by the question.
Hence growth and reproduction is the best answer.
explanation.simple_explanation: A sprouting tuber shows that even when stored, living tissues can resume growth and produce a new plant. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following is the best reason why seeds are regarded as living?

options.B: They can germinate under suitable conditions
options.C: They remain dry for long periods
options.D: They are always visible to the naked eye

answer_text: They can germinate under suitable conditions

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Dormant seeds are living because their cells are alive and capable of resuming metabolism and growth during germination.
steps: A seed may appear inactive, but its embryo is alive.
When moisture, oxygen, and suitable temperature are available, it germinates.
That ability to resume life processes shows it is living.
Colour, dryness, or visibility do not define life.
explanation.final_answer: B. They can germinate under suitable conditions
explanation.simple_explanation: JAMB sometimes tests dormant life forms. A dormant seed is still alive because it can germinate. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: The removal of undigested food through the anus is known as

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Egestion is the elimination of undigested food, while excretion is the removal of metabolic waste made by cells.
steps: Undigested food has never become part of the body's metabolism.
It is therefore not a metabolic waste product.
Its removal through the anus is called egestion.
This is different from excretion of urea, CO₂, excess salts, and water.
explanation.simple_explanation: Many students mix up egestion and excretion, so this is an important exam point. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following activities best demonstrates irritability in animals?

options.A: A lizard basking in the sun after a cold night

answer_text: A lizard basking in the sun after a cold night

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Irritability is response to environmental changes; basking is a behavioural response to temperature.
steps: The environment becomes cold at night, lowering the lizard's body temperature.
The lizard responds by moving into sunlight to warm itself.
That is a response to a stimulus.
Chewing cud is feeding, laying eggs is reproduction, and increasing size is growth.
explanation.final_answer: A. A lizard basking in the sun after a cold night
explanation.simple_explanation: Response to temperature, light, touch, or chemicals is a common way JAMB tests irritability. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following life processes is directly concerned with the release of energy from food?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Respiration releases usable energy from food molecules inside living cells.
steps: Food contains chemical energy.
Respiration breaks down food molecules to release that energy for body activities.
Excretion removes wastes, reproduction forms new individuals, and locomotion is movement.
Thus respiration is the correct process.
explanation.simple_explanation: Even when oxygen is not mentioned, remember respiration is the life process for energy release. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: A green plant kept in darkness for a long time may die mainly because it cannot

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Light is required for photosynthesis, through which green plants manufacture food.
steps: Plants respire both day and night, but they need food reserves to keep respiring.
In prolonged darkness, photosynthesis stops or becomes grossly reduced.
If stored food is exhausted, the plant cannot maintain life.
So the main problem is failure to manufacture enough food.
explanation.simple_explanation: Plants do respire in darkness, but without continued food production they eventually weaken and may die. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following combinations is correct for all living things?

options.A: Movement from place to place, photosynthesis and reproduction
options.D: Locomotion, feeding on solid food and sensitivity

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Some life processes are universal, while others occur only in certain groups of organisms.
steps: All living things respire, grow, and respond to stimuli.
Not all living things photosynthesize, move from place to place, or feed on solid food.
Many living things are not green.
Therefore the universal combination is growth, respiration, and irritability.
explanation.simple_explanation: When JAMB asks for processes shown by all living things, avoid options containing special features of only plants or only animals. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following best shows that fungi are living things?

options.A: They are found on damp bread
options.B: They can grow and reproduce by spores

answer_text: They can grow and reproduce by spores

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Living things are identified by the processes they perform, not just by where they are found or what they lack.
steps: Fungi increase in size and produce spores for reproduction.
Those are clear biological activities.
Being soft or lacking chlorophyll does not by itself prove life.
Thus growth and spore formation give the best evidence.
explanation.final_answer: B. They can grow and reproduce by spores
explanation.simple_explanation: The strongest proof of life is always a genuine life process. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following statements about movement in living things is correct?

options.B: Plants do not show any form of movement
options.C: Movement may be internal or external in living things
options.D: All movements in organisms are locomotion

answer_text: Movement may be internal or external in living things

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Living things may show movement of the whole body or movement of parts and materials within the body.
steps: Animals often show locomotion from place to place.
Plants also show movement such as growth curvature, opening of flowers, and cytoplasmic streaming.
Movement of blood, food, or cell contents also counts as biological movement.
So movement may be internal or external.
explanation.final_answer: C. Movement may be internal or external in living things
explanation.simple_explanation: Do not reduce movement only to walking or swimming; biology includes internal movement too. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: An organism is said to be metabolically active when it is

options.A: changing food materials through chemical reactions in its body
options.B: moving very fast from place to place

answer_text: changing food materials through chemical reactions in its body

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Metabolism refers to all the chemical reactions going on in a living organism.
steps: Food is broken down and new substances are built up through body chemistry.
These reactions include respiration, synthesis, and breakdown processes.
Fast movement or green colour is not the meaning of metabolism.
Therefore metabolic activity means internal chemical reactions are occurring.
explanation.final_answer: A. changing food materials through chemical reactions in its body
explanation.simple_explanation: Metabolism is a core concept behind many life processes, especially nutrition, respiration, and growth. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following is a non-living thing showing a feature that can be mistaken for growth?

options.B: A young chick increasing in mass
options.C: A sugar crystal increasing in size in solution

answer_text: A sugar crystal increasing in size in solution

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Some non-living objects may increase in size, but not by true biological growth.
steps: A crystal can become larger when more particles deposit on it from a solution.
That enlargement is external accretion, not cell division or synthesis of protoplasm.
The other options involve true living growth.
Hence the crystal is the correct answer.
explanation.final_answer: C. A sugar crystal increasing in size in solution
explanation.simple_explanation: This question checks whether you can separate true growth from mere increase in size. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following would be the best test for determining whether an unknown object is living?

options.B: Observe whether it performs several life processes
options.D: Observe whether it can remain dry

answer_text: Observe whether it performs several life processes

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: No single superficial feature proves life as reliably as the performance of life processes.
steps: Colour, weight, or dryness may occur in both living and non-living things.
A dependable test is to look for activities such as growth, respiration, excretion, response, and reproduction.
Living things are functionally organized and active biologically.
Therefore observing several life processes gives the best answer.
explanation.final_answer: B. Observe whether it performs several life processes
explanation.simple_explanation: In exams, functional evidence is stronger than appearance-based evidence. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: The process by which organisms maintain themselves and carry out their activities is generally called

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Life processes are the various activities that keep organisms alive and functioning.
steps: These include nutrition, respiration, growth, excretion, movement, sensitivity, and reproduction.
Ecology studies relationships with the environment, taxonomy deals with classification, and evolution concerns long-term change.
The question asks for the general activities of living things.
So the correct expression is life processes.
explanation.simple_explanation: This is a foundational definition question, but JAMB often hides it among related terms. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following best explains why a hibernating animal is still considered living?

options.B: Its life processes continue at a reduced rate

answer_text: Its life processes continue at a reduced rate

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Some organisms enter dormant or low-activity states, but their basic life processes still continue slowly.
steps: During hibernation, breathing, heartbeat, and metabolism are greatly reduced, not absent.
The animal still remains alive and can return to active life later.
If chemical activity stopped completely, the organism would be dead.
So reduced life processes explain why it is still living.
explanation.final_answer: B. Its life processes continue at a reduced rate
explanation.simple_explanation: Dormancy does not mean death; it means lowered activity. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following is most directly involved in the removal of carbon(IV) oxide from the human body?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Carbon(IV) oxide is a metabolic waste product and its removal is classified as excretion.
steps: Cells produce carbon(IV) oxide during respiration.
The lungs remove it from the body.
Because it is a waste product of metabolism, its removal is excretion.
Therefore excretion is the best answer.
explanation.simple_explanation: The gas is produced in respiration but removed by excretion; JAMB can test either angle. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: The opening of flower buds in the morning and closing at night is mainly an example of

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Plants can move parts of their bodies in response to environmental changes such as light and temperature.
steps: Flower parts may open and close according to day-night conditions.
That is a response to external stimuli and therefore a form of movement or irritability.
It is not directly reproduction, though flowers are reproductive organs.
Hence movement in response to stimuli is best.
explanation.final_answer: B. movement in response to stimuli
explanation.simple_explanation: Plant movement questions often test whether you know that plants do move, though not usually by locomotion. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following statements is correct about respiration and breathing?

options.A: They mean exactly the same thing
options.B: Breathing is a physical process, while respiration is a chemical process in cells

answer_text: Breathing is a physical process, while respiration is a chemical process in cells

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Breathing is the exchange of gases with the environment, whereas respiration is the release of energy in cells.
steps: Breathing or ventilation moves air in and out of respiratory surfaces.
Respiration occurs inside cells and involves chemical breakdown of food to release energy.
So the two terms are related but not identical.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.final_answer: B. Breathing is a physical process, while respiration is a chemical process in cells
explanation.simple_explanation: This distinction is extremely important in UTME Biology. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: A response of plant roots growing downward into the soil is evidence of

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Geotropism is the growth response of plant parts to gravity.
steps: Roots usually grow toward gravitational pull, so they are positively geotropic.
Phototropism is response to light; hydrotropism is response to water.
The question asks specifically about downward growth.
That indicates geotropism.
explanation.simple_explanation: Although this belongs to plant response, it also shows the general life characteristic of irritability. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following is an example of secretion rather than excretion?

options.A: Release of bile by the liver
options.C: Loss of carbon(IV) oxide through the lungs

answer_text: Release of bile by the liver

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Secretion is the production of useful substances for body functions, while excretion is removal of metabolic waste.
steps: Bile helps digestion and is useful in the body, so its release is secretion.
Urea, carbon(IV) oxide, and excess salts are wastes removed from the body.
Those are excretory products.
Hence option A is correct.
explanation.final_answer: A. Release of bile by the liver
explanation.simple_explanation: Learning to separate secretion from excretion helps to avoid common exam traps. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: A flame that burns brightly, consumes oxygen and releases energy is still not considered living because it does not

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Some non-living systems resemble living things in one or two ways, but they lack the organized cellular basis of life and heredity.
steps: Fire may seem to feed, spread, and use oxygen.
However, it has no cells, no genetic material, and no true biological reproduction.
Living things are built of cells and pass hereditary information to offspring.
Therefore lack of cellular organization and heredity shows it is not living.
explanation.final_answer: C. maintain cellular organization and heredity
explanation.simple_explanation: JAMB sometimes uses borderline examples like fire or crystals to test deep understanding of life. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: The ability of an organism to produce young ones that resemble it is called

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Reproduction is the life process by which organisms give rise to offspring of the same kind.
steps: The keyword here is producing young ones.
Adaptation relates to survival features, growth is increase in size, and nutrition is feeding.
Only reproduction fits the statement exactly.
So option C is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Watch out for straightforward reproduction questions mixed with other life processes. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following life processes is most directly responsible for continuity of a species?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: A species continues from generation to generation only when organisms reproduce.
steps: An individual may stay alive by respiration and excretion.
However, survival of the species depends on producing offspring.
Therefore continuity of a species is tied most directly to reproduction.
That makes option C correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Many life processes preserve the individual, but reproduction preserves the species. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Characteristics of living things
question: Which of the following would be least useful in deciding whether a newly discovered object is alive?

options.A: Whether it can respond to changes in light
options.B: Whether it shows internal chemical activity
options.C: Whether it has an attractive shape
options.D: Whether it can reproduce or grow

answer_text: Whether it has an attractive shape

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on characteristics of living things.
explanation.principle: Appearance alone is a weak indicator of life compared with true biological functions.
steps: Response, metabolism, growth, and reproduction are all strong biological criteria.
An attractive or regular shape can occur in non-living objects such as crystals or machines.
Therefore shape is least useful in deciding whether something is alive.
Option C is the best answer.
explanation.final_answer: C. Whether it has an attractive shape
explanation.simple_explanation: Functional evidence beats superficial appearance in biology questions. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Cell structure and functions of cell components
question: The basic structural and functional unit of life is the

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on cell structure and functions of cell components.
explanation.principle: All living organisms are made of cells, and the cell is the smallest unit that can carry out life processes.
steps: Tissues are groups of similar cells, and organs are made of tissues.
The nucleus is only one part of the cell.
The complete basic unit is the cell itself.
Therefore option B is correct.
explanation.simple_explanation: Whenever JAMB asks for the fundamental unit of life, the answer is cell. To understand this topic better, check our "Tutorial section" for a fuller beginner-friendly lesson.

subtopic: Cell structure and functions of cell components
question: Which cell component controls the activities of the cell?

explanation.question_restate: We are to identify the best answer to this JAMB-style Biology question on cell structure and functions of cell components.
explanation.principle: The nucleus acts as the control centre because it contains genetic material and regulates cellular activities.
steps: The nucleus houses chromosomes and genes.
These influence protein synthesis and many cell functions.
The membrane regulates entry and exit, but does not serve as the main control centre.
Hence the nucleus is correct.

subtopic: Levels of organization: cell, tissue, organ, system and organism
